title: Catch Semantics, Collection Identity, Randomness, and Deterministic Evidence · Execution Trace: Pokedex Go Catch Transition Tests
summary: Catch uses a hidden global random source, stores partial response pointers, duplicates the same Pokemon unpredictably, and makes tests depend on luck and request timing. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go catch transition tests increment in deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Prove success, miss, duplicate, lookup failure, cancellation, seeded boundary, and unchanged-collection failure cases.
Store a deliberately owned domain value rather than a transport pointer or cache alias and define mutation policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
